title: Using Library Databases for Academic Research
text: The university library provides access to curated academic databases containing peer-reviewed journals
allowed_use: and scholarly articles. Students can use subject filters
language: and keyword tools to narrow search results to credible sources relevant to their field of study. These databases are a far more reliable starting point for academic papers than general internet searches.
column13: General academic guidance and research tips

title: Borrowing Materials Through Interlibrary Loan
text: When a required book or article is not available in the university library's own collection
source_id: students can request it through the Interlibrary Loan (ILL) service. This service connects your institution to external libraries and allows you to borrow materials at no cost. Requests typically take a few days to process and arrive either digitally or as physical copies.
blocked_use: Academic research support and resource access

title: Booking a Study Room at the Library
text: Most university libraries offer group and individual study rooms that students can reserve in advance. These quiet
source_id: dedicated spaces are ideal for focused exam preparation
blocked_use: or collaborative work. Students can check room availability and book a slot through the library's online reservation portal or by visiting the front desk.

title: Getting One-on-One Help from a Subject Librarian
text: Subject librarians are available for one-on-one research consultations to help students identify the right databases
allowed_use: and manage citations effectively. These appointments are free and can be scheduled online or in person. Students working on theses
language: or major papers especially benefit from these sessions.

title: Accessing E-Books and Digital Archives Remotely
text: University libraries offer remote access to electronic books
allowed_use: and digital archives through student login portals. Students can access these resources from home using their university credentials
blocked_use: making research and study possible outside of library hours. Many universities also provide LinkedIn Learning and other digital skill-development platforms under the same login.

title: The Modern Library as a 21st-Century Learning Hub
text: The Grover M. Hermann Library at University of the Cumberlands has been transformed into a $7.5 million 21st-century learning center featuring group and individual study rooms
allowed_use: and a makerspace for current students. A coffee shop and lounge are also part of the library space. The facility is connected to the Rollins Family Learning Center which houses the Patriot Student Success Center.
language: Campus facility awareness and academic planning

title: CARE Program: Confidential Support for Survivors
text: UCLA's CARE (Campus Assault Resources and Education) program is a safe and confidential resource for students who are survivors of sexual assault
language: or sexual harassment. CARE Advocates help students access support
column14: and connect with medical or legal services — all without requiring a formal report to be filed. Contact CARE at (310) 206-2465 or advocate@careprogram.ucla.edu.

title: Reporting a Student in Distress – UCLA CRT
text: UCLA's Consultation and Response Team (CRT) is a professional team that responds to reports about students who may be experiencing a mental health crisis or safety concern. It includes representatives from the Dean of Students office
blocked_use: and UCLA Police. Case Managers can meet directly with students or advise faculty and staff on how to help. Email CRTeam@ucla.edu during business hours or call the 24-hour CAPS line at (310) 825-0768 for after-hours support.
column12: Mental health referral and distress support

title: 24-Hour Mental Health and Crisis Support at UCLA
text: For immediate mental health emergencies at UCLA
source_id: students can call the Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) 24-hour crisis line at (310) 825-0768 at any time
allowed_use: including nights and weekends. For physical emergencies or situations involving immediate danger
blocked_use: students should call the UCLA Police Department at (310) 825-1491 or dial 911. These services are available around the clock to all enrolled UCLA students.
column12: Crisis response and immediate safety guidance

title: Financial Crisis Support Through UCLA ECRT
text: The Economic Crisis Response Team (ECRT) at UCLA supports students who are facing serious financial hardship that threatens their ability to continue their studies. The team works compassionately and discreetly to connect students with emergency funds
allowed_use: and provide individualized guidance. Students experiencing financial crises can email ecr@saonet.ucla.edu or call (310) 206-1189 to get help.
language: Financial distress referral and emergency support

title: How to Report Sexual Harassment Under Title IX
text: Students who have experienced sexual harassment at UCLA can contact the Title IX Office at (310) 206-3417 or titleix@conet.ucla.edu. The Title IX Office maintains privacy while fulfilling its obligation to respond appropriately. Students unsure about filing a formal report may first reach out to confidential resources such as the CARE Program
allowed_use: or the Rape Treatment Center at Santa Monica-UCLA Medical Center — none of whom are required to report incidents to the university.
language: Survivor support and formal reporting guidance

title: Emergency Mental Health Services at UVA
text: All UVA students are eligible for emergency mental health assessments and crisis consultations at no cost. For life-threatening emergencies
source_id: students should call 911 immediately — UVA phones no longer require dialing 9 before 911. The CASS on Call service provides 24-hour crisis management and follow-up support and can be reached at (434) 924-7166. These services are available to all enrolled UVA students at any time.
blocked_use: Crisis navigation and emergency mental health access

title: Reporting Crimes and Safety Concerns at UVA
text: The UVA Police Department is located at 2304 Ivy Road and can be reached for non-emergency matters at (434) 924-7166. Students can also report crimes
allowed_use: and access the campus crime log through the UVA Police website. For immediate danger
blocked_use: dial 911. University Ambassadors — trained safety volunteers — also patrol the area near the Corner and can escort students home upon request.
column12: Campus safety awareness and crime reporting

topic: Safe Transportation and Campus Safety Resources
title: Safe Transportation and Night Safety Options at UVA
text: UVA Student Affairs offers safe transportation options for students traveling around Grounds
source_id: including late-night and on-demand services designed to reduce personal safety risks. University Ambassadors in bright yellow attire patrol areas around the Corner and can walk students to their destination. Students can call the Public Safety Substation at (434) 984-7622 ext. 406 or drop in at 1413 University Ave to request a safe walk.

title: Academic Advising vs. Tutoring: What's the Difference?
text: Academic advising helps students plan their overall academic journey — including choosing courses
language: focuses on helping students with specific subjects or assignments
column12: such as preparing for a math exam or completing a lab report. Both services are typically available for free through the student affairs or academic support office and can be accessed by appointment or walk-in.

title: Disability Services: Getting Academic Accommodations
text: More than one in five undergraduate students have a disability
source_id: and universities provide dedicated disability and accessibility offices to support them. These offices coordinate academic accommodations such as extended test time
blocked_use: and accessible course materials in partnership with faculty. Students should register with their institution's disability office early in the semester to ensure timely accommodations are in place.

title: Support Services Tailored for International Students
text: International students face unique challenges including culture shock
allowed_use: and immigration regulations. Many universities provide dedicated international student support services that assist with housing guidance
column12: and integration programs. Students are encouraged to connect with the international student office early in their first semester for orientation resources and ongoing support.
column15: Immigration legal advice or visa application processing

title: Emergency Financial Aid for Students in Crisis
text: CityU of Seattle offers Emergency Aid for currently enrolled students facing unexpected financial hardship that threatens their ability to continue studying. Eligible scenarios include involuntary job loss
language: and family travel emergencies. Students must have a minimum 2.0 GPA (3.0 for graduates)
column13: and FAFSA on file. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis through the emergency aid application portal.
column15: Financial hardship referral and emergency funding guidance

title: Support for First-Generation and Low-Income Students
text: First-generation and low-income students often lack the academic networks and financial safety nets available to other students. Universities offer specialized support including welcome programs
blocked_use: and family orientation events. Staff can also help connect students experiencing housing insecurity or food insecurity with community support programs and on-campus resources.
column12: Peer support awareness and equity-focused resource navigation
column13: Specific financial planning or investment advice

title: How to Write a Strong Resume or CV for Jobs
text: A resume is a formal document that highlights your relevant education
blocked_use: and achievements for job or internship applications. A curriculum vitae (CV) is used in academic and research settings and is typically several pages long. University Career Services provides sample resumes
column12: and recorded resume workshops to help students prepare polished application materials. Students can also schedule appointments for personalized feedback.
column14: Career preparation and job application support

title: Free Career Assessments to Discover Your Strengths
text: University Career Services offers free career assessment tools to help students identify their interests
blocked_use: and career fit. Free tools include Focus 2 (linked to university majors)
column12: and Holland Codes exploration worksheets. Paid assessments such as the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) and Strong Interest Inventory are available for $15 with a career counselor pre-assessment appointment.

title: Preparing for Internships and Job Applications
text: University Career Services supports students in preparing for internships and jobs through mock interview sessions
allowed_use: and LinkedIn profile reviews. The Career Development Action Plan and Checklist helps students build competencies at each stage — from exploring interests (0–29 credits) to empowering employment (60+ credits). Career fairs and networking events are also hosted throughout the year to connect students directly with employers.

title: Planning for Graduate and Professional School Applications
text: Students considering graduate or professional school can access dedicated resources through University Career Services
blocked_use: and selecting programs. Career counselors can review application materials and provide strategic advice. The Pre-Law Checklist and academic interview question guides are among the resources available for students pursuing advanced degrees.
column12: Graduate school preparation and career planning
column13: Specific admissions decisions or legal advice

title: Exploring Career Paths Based on Your Major
text: UC Berkeley's Career Engagement portal provides a comprehensive resource linking academic majors to typical career areas and industries. Students can browse alphabetically to find career areas commonly associated with their major and explore related fields. The First Destination Survey shows where past graduates have gone
source_id: providing realistic insight into career outcomes and helping students make informed decisions about their professional paths.
blocked_use: Career exploration and academic major guidance

title: Getting Involved: Student Organizations and Campus Activities
text: Joining student organizations
allowed_use: and volunteer groups is one of the most effective ways to develop leadership skills
language: and enrich the university experience. Most universities offer a wide range of clubs — from academic and cultural societies to professional associations and recreational groups. Getting involved in campus life also demonstrates initiative and teamwork on future resumes and graduate school applications.

title: How Peer Mentorship Programs Help Students Succeed
text: Peer mentorship programs connect newer students with more experienced peers who can provide guidance on academic challenges
allowed_use: and professional development. These programs reduce feelings of isolation
language: and help students build confidence during their first year or during difficult academic periods. Many universities offer both formal peer mentor matching and informal mentoring through student clubs and honors programs.

title: Setting Up and Managing Your University NetID
text: Every UIC student is automatically assigned a NetID upon admission
source_id: which serves as the login credential for Blackboard
column13: and LinkedIn Learning. Students can activate their NetID through the university portal and manage their password
column15: and enroll in 2-Factor Authentication via the NetID Center. Passwords must meet university complexity requirements to protect account security.
column17: IT onboarding and account access guidance

title: Connecting to UIC-WiFi on Windows and macOS
text: UIC students
allowed_use: and staff can connect to the UIC-WiFi network using their NetID and password. On Windows
blocked_use: students select UIC-WiFi from network settings and enter their credentials. On macOS
language: the same steps apply via System Preferences. Visitors may use UIC-Guest or Eduroam. When a password is changed
column12: students must forget and re-add the network using updated credentials. Detailed device-specific guides are available on the UIC IT Knowledge Base.
column14: IT setup and campus connectivity support

title: Accessing Your UIC Email via Outlook
text: UIC students use Microsoft Exchange and Outlook as their official email and calendaring platform. Email can be accessed on the web at outlook.uic.edu using the full UIC email address and NetID password. Duo 2-Factor Authentication is required to log in. Students with a Microsoft 365 A5 license (assigned to all UIC students) may also install the Outlook desktop app on Windows or macOS and configure the app on iOS or Android devices.
allowed_use: Student email setup and IT account access

title: Securing Your Account with Duo 2-Factor Authentication
text: UIC requires all students to use Duo 2-Factor Authentication (2FA) when logging into university systems. 2FA adds a second layer of security by requiring identity verification through a mobile device after entering your NetID and password. This protects university data and prevents unauthorized access. Students can enroll in 2FA
allowed_use: and manage settings through the NetID Center; international students can also enable 2FA while outside the USA.
language: Cybersecurity awareness and IT account protection

topic: Virtual Computer Lab and Software Access
title: Accessing University Software Through the Virtual Computer Lab
text: UIC's Virtual Computer Lab (VCL) allows students to access licensed software remotely from any device
source_id: without needing to install programs locally. Available software includes MATLAB
column16: and Wolfram Mathematica. Students can connect through a web browser or the VCL desktop application. This is especially useful for students who need specialized academic tools but do not own high-performance computers.
column18: Academic IT tools and software access
column19: Piracy or software installation from unofficial sources

title: Printing on Campus Using the Wepa System
text: UIC uses the Wepa printing system for on-campus printing needs. Students can upload documents via the Wepa app or website
source_id: then release print jobs at any Wepa-enabled printer across campus. The system supports printing from mobile devices
blocked_use: and computer labs. Students receive a printing credit allocation each semester and can add additional funds if needed. Wepa stations are located in libraries

topic: UIC IT Help Center and Technical Support
title: Getting IT Help Through the UIC Help Center
text: The UIC Help Center at help.uic.edu is the central platform for submitting IT support tickets
blocked_use: and accessing how-to guides through the Knowledge Base. For in-person or virtual support
language: the Technical Helpdesk assists with accounts
column17: and other university technology services. Students can also ask questions directly through the Ask an IT Question portal.

title: Requesting Transcripts and Enrollment Verification
text: The Office of the Registrar is the primary source for all information regarding attendance
column12: and enrollment verification. Students can request official transcripts
column14: and access academic catalogs through the Registrar's portal. The office is located on the first floor of the Gatliff Building and can be reached at registrar@ucumberlands.edu or 606.539.4316.

title: Registrar Services: Course Registration and Graduation
text: Through the Office of the Registrar
language: and track degree completion requirements. All essential forms
column13: and procedural information are available through the Registrar's online portal. Students who need in-person assistance can visit the office during business hours or contact the team by email or phone.

title: CARE Program: Education Advocacy and Healing for Survivors
text: UCLA's CARE Program champions a campus free from sexual and gender-based violence through three interconnected service areas: community education
allowed_use: and healing-focused programs. CARE Advocates provide survivor-centered support including guidance

title: Rape Treatment Center: Emergency Sexual Assault Support
text: If a student has recently experienced sexual violence
source_id: the Santa Monica Rape Treatment Center (RTC) is available 24 hours a day
allowed_use: 7 days a week. Survivors can obtain a free forensic exam (for evidence collection) up to five days after an assault by calling (424) 259-7208. The RTC also provides free trauma therapy regardless of whether a forensic exam is obtained. For immediate danger

topic: Case Management Services for Students in Crisis
title: Case Management: Personalized Support for Students Facing Crises
text: UCLA Case Managers are available to assist students struggling with mental or physical health challenges
language: and help students create a plan for next steps. Case Managers typically meet with students one to four times and can follow up by email or phone throughout the academic year. To initiate contact

title: CityU Counseling Center: Affordable Online Counseling
text: The City University Counseling Center is a tele-health online facility offering individual
allowed_use: and group counseling on a sliding scale of $5 to $25 per session. Washington state residents enrolled in K-12 or higher education may be seen at no charge. Services cover stress
column17: email counseling@cityu.edu. Hours are Monday to Thursday 8 AM to 6 PM and Friday 9 AM to 5 PM.
column19: Mental health counseling referral and access guidance

title: Writing Centers: One-on-One Help with Papers and Language
text: Many universities operate dedicated writing centers staffed by tutors who specialize in grammar
blocked_use: and academic writing conventions. Students can bring a paper in any stage of development for one-on-one feedback sessions. Language centers
column14: and language skills — particularly valuable for ESL and international students.

title: Building a LinkedIn Profile and Professional Network
text: Creating a LinkedIn profile is a key step in career development
source_id: allowing students to connect with peers
language: and alumni in their field of interest. Career Services provides guidance on building an effective profile and connecting with relevant professional groups. Industry knowledge can also be maintained through LinkedIn by following companies
column13: and professional associations in your chosen career area.

title: Making the Most of University Career Fairs
text: Career fairs organized by University Career Services allow students to meet employers
allowed_use: and discover job and internship opportunities in one setting. Students are encouraged to attend career fairs from their freshman year to build confidence and connections early. Bringing a polished resume and practicing a brief self-introduction (elevator pitch) beforehand significantly improves the experience.

title: Accessing Professional Clothing Through the Career Closet
text: Many university career services offices operate a Career Closet where students can borrow professional attire for interviews
allowed_use: and internship orientations. This service ensures that cost is not a barrier to presenting professionally during job searches. Students typically fill out a survey or make an appointment to access available clothing items at no charge.

title: Planning Your Graduate School Application Timeline
text: Applying to graduate school requires careful planning starting at least 12 months before your intended start date. Key steps include researching programs and testing requirements (GRE
column12: and tracking individual program deadlines. University Career Services offers program-specific checklists such as the Pre-Law Checklist to help students stay organized throughout the process.

title: Finding Open Computer Labs on Campus
text: Technology Solutions at UIC manages both teaching labs (reserved for class use) and open access computer labs available to all students during operating hours. Open labs are located in GH 306
allowed_use: and SELE 2265. Labs are typically open from 7:30 AM to 9:00 PM on weekdays. Wepa print stations are available adjacent to most labs for student printing needs.

title: Getting Help with Blackboard at UIC
text: Blackboard Learn is UIC's official Learning Management System (LMS) for course materials

title: Staying Safe Online: Cybersecurity Tips for University Students
text: The university IT department will never ask for your password by email
allowed_use: or chat. Students should use strong
blocked_use: unique passwords for their NetID and change them regularly. Enabling Duo 2-Factor Authentication adds a critical layer of protection against unauthorized account access. Phishing emails often impersonate IT staff or university offices — when in doubt
language: report suspicious messages to IT Security via help.uic.edu and do not click any links.

title: Career Development in Your First Two Years of University
text: The University of Houston Career Development Checklist recommends that freshmen and sophomores focus on exploring self
allowed_use: and career fields by completing the Focus 2 assessment
language: exploring career fields through volunteering or job shadowing
column12: and gaining experience through part-time work or internships. Students should also log into Cougar Pathway (AccessUH) to schedule a career counselor appointment and discover on-campus job opportunities.

title: Career Transition Steps for Juniors and Seniors
text: As students approach graduation
